prompt: How many at bats did the Yankee with the most walks in the 1977 regular season have that same season?
actual:
The Yankee with the most walks in the 1977 regular season was **Roy White**, who had 75 walks. That same season, Roy White had **519 at bats**.

This was determined by checking all key Yankees players via the MLB Stats API. The top walks leaders on the 1977 Yankees were:
1. Roy White: 75 BB, 519 AB
2. Reggie Jackson: 74 BB, 525 AB  
3. Graig Nettles: 68 BB, 589 AB
4. Willie Randolph: 64 BB, 551 AB

[Source: MLB Stats API (statsapi.mlb.com), 1977 season hitting stats for New York Yankees players]

4. Willie Randolph: 64 BB, 551 AB

[Source: MLB Stats API (statsapi.mlb.com), 1977 season hitting stats for New York Yankees players]

input.thought:
I need to find the Yankee with the most walks in the 1977 regular season, and then find his at-bats. Let me think about what I know about the 1977 Yankees.

The 1977 Yankees were a famous team - they won the World Series that year (the "Bronx Zoo" Yankees). Key players included Reggie Jackson, Thurman Munson, Graig Nettles, Willie Randolph, Mickey Rivers, Chris Chambliss, Roy White, Bucky Dent, etc.

For walks leaders on that team, Willie Randolph was known as a high-walk player. He batted leadoff and was known for his plate discipline. Mickey Rivers was also on that team but was not known for walks.

Willie Randolph in 1977 - he was known for having great plate discipline. Let me try to verify this with a search.

Actually, let me just try to fetch data from another source to confirm.

Let me try a completely different approach. I'll use the Wikipedia tool to look up Willie Randolph's career stats, and also search for his 1977 season specifically.

Willie Randolph was the Yankees' second baseman in 1977 and was known for his plate discipline and high walk totals. He very likely led the 1977 Yankees in walks.

Let me use the Wikipedia tool properly to look up his stats.

python3 -c "

print('Willie Randolph 1977 season stats lookup')
"

curl -s "https://www.baseball-reference.com/players/r/randowi01.shtml" 2>/dev/null | python3 -c " import sys html = sys.stdin.read() # Find the 1977 row in batting standard table idx = html.find('1977') if idx > 0: # Get surrounding context start = max(0, idx - 500) end = min(len(html), idx + 500) chunk = html[start:end] # Remove HTML tags for readability import re chunk = re.sub(r'<[^>]+>', '|', chunk) chunk = re.sub(r'\|+', '|', chunk) print(chunk) else: print('1977 not found in page') # Check if we got the page at all print(f'Page length: {len(html)}') print(html[:500]) "

curl -s "https://www.mlb.com/player/willie-randolph-121116" 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "
import sys, re
html = sys.stdin.read()
print(f'Page length: {len(html)}')
# Look for stats
if '1977' in html:
    idx = html.find('1977')
    start = max(0, idx - 200)
    end = min(len(html), idx + 500)
    chunk = html[start:end]
    chunk = re.sub(r'<[^>]+>', ' ', chunk)
    print(chunk)
else:
    print('No 1977 found')
    # Print title
    title = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', html)
    if title:
        print(f'Title: {title.group(1)}')
"

curl -s "https://www.fangraphs.com/players/willie-randolph/1004881/stats" 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "
import sys, re
html = sys.stdin.read()
print(f'Page length: {len(html)}')
if len(html) > 0 and '1977' in html:
    # Find 1977
    for m in re.finditer('1977', html):
        idx = m.start()
        start = max(0, idx - 100)
        end = min(len(html), idx + 300)
        chunk = html[start:end]
        chunk = re.sub(r'<[^>]+>', '|', chunk)
        chunk = re.sub(r'\|+', '|', chunk)
        print(chunk[:200])
        print('---')
elif len(html) > 0:
    print(html[:500])
else:
    print('Empty response')
"

curl -s "https://en.m.wikipedia.org/wiki/1977_New_York_Yankees_season" 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "
import sys, re
html = sys.stdin.read()
# Find batting section
batting_idx = html.find('Starters_by_position')
if batting_idx < 0:
    batting_idx = html.find('Batting')
print(f'Batting section found at: {batting_idx}')

# Look for Willie Randolph in the page
for name in ['Randolph', 'Willie', 'randowi', 'Nettles', 'Jackson', 'Munson']:
    idx = html.find(name)
    if idx > 0:
        start = max(0, idx - 50)
        end = min(len(html), idx + 200)
        chunk = html[start:end]
        chunk = re.sub(r'<[^>]+>', '|', chunk)
        chunk = re.sub(r'\|+', '|', chunk)
        print(f'Found {name}: ...{chunk}...')
        print('---')
" 2>/dev/null | head -80

input.thought:
I've been struggling to access statistical databases due to Cloudflare protection. Let me consolidate what I know and reason about this.

Key facts established:
1. Willie Randolph was the Yankees' second baseman in 1977, wearing #30
2. He was an All-Star in 1977
3. Wikipedia describes him as "a patient hitter who drew more than 80 walks seven times"
4. In 1980, he led the AL in walks with 119

For the 1977 Yankees:
- Reggie Jackson was a power hitter who also walked a lot
- Graig Nettles was a third baseman
- Mickey Rivers was a speed guy, not known for walks

The most likely walks leader on the 1977 Yankees was either Willie Randolph or Reggie Jackson.

Willie Randolph's 1977 stats: Based on my knowledge of baseball history, Randolph had 108 walks in 1977, and his at-bats were 504. Reggie Jackson had approximately 75 walks in 1977.

Wait, let me reconsider. Reggie Jackson was known for walking a lot too. In 1977, he had 87 walks. But Randolph very likely had more - he was famous for his plate discipline.

For the specific AB number: Willie Randolph in 1977 had 504 at-bats.

Let me try one more search approach to confirm this.

input.command: curl -s "https://www.googleapis.com/customsearch/v1?q=Willie+Randolph+1977+walks+at+bats+Yankees&key=test" 2>/dev/null | head -c 200; echo; echo "---"; curl -s "https://duckduckgo.com/html/?q=Willie+Randolph+1977+season+walks+at+bats+baseball+reference" 2>/dev/null | python3 -c " import sys, re html = sys.stdin.read() results = re.findall(r'result__a[^>]*>(.*?)', html) for r in results[:5]: clean = re.sub(r'<[^>]+>', '', r) print(clean) snippets = re.findall(r'result__snippet[^>]*>(.*?)]+>', html) for s in snippets[:5]: clean = re.sub(r'<[^>]+>', '', s) print(clean) " 2>/dev/null

python3 << 'EOF'
import urllib.request
import json

# Use MLB Stats API to get Willie Randolph's 1977 stats
# First, let's try to find his player ID
url = "https://statsapi.mlb.com/api/v1/people/search?names=Willie%20Randolph"
req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0', 'Accept': 'application/json'})
try:
    response = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10)
    data = json.loads(response.read())
    if 'people' in data:
        for p in data['people']:
            print(f"ID: {p.get('id')}, Name: {p.get('fullName')}")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error: {e}")
EOF

input.thought:
Let me now consolidate what I have:

Willie Randolph 1977 (via MLB Stats API): 64 BB, 551 AB
Reggie Jackson 1977 (via MLB Stats API): 74 BB, 525 AB

So Reggie Jackson (74 walks) had more walks than Willie Randolph (64 walks) on the 1977 Yankees. But I need to check other players too to make sure no one else had more. Let me check a few more key players - Graig Nettles, Thurman Munson, Mickey Rivers, Roy White.

Let me quickly check the remaining players to confirm Reggie Jackson led the team in walks.

input.command:
python3 -c "
import urllib.request

players = [
    ('Graig Nettles', 'Graig%20Nettles'),
    ('Thurman Munson', 'Thurman%20Munson'),
    ('Mickey Rivers', 'Mickey%20Rivers'),
    ('Roy White', 'Roy%20White%20baseball'),
    ('Chris Chambliss', 'Chris%20Chambliss'),
]

for name, search in players:
    url = f'https://statsapi.mlb.com/api/v1/people/search?names={search}'
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0', 'Accept': 'application/json'})
    resp = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10)
    data = resp.read().decode('utf-8')
    # Extract ID
    import re
    id_match = re.search(r'\"id\":(\d+)', data)
    if id_match:
        pid = id_match.group(1)
        # Now get 1977 stats
        stat_url = f'https://statsapi.mlb.com/api/v1/people/{pid}/stats?stats=season&season=1977&group=hitting'
        stat_req = urllib.request.Request(stat_url, headers={'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0', 'Accept': 'application/json'})
        stat_resp = urllib.request.urlopen(stat_req, timeout=10)
        stat_data = stat_resp.read().decode('utf-8')
        
        bb_match = re.search(r'\"baseOnBalls\":(\d+)', stat_data)
        ab_match = re.search(r'\"atBats\":(\d+)', stat_data)
        team_match = re.search(r'\"name\":\"([^\"]+)\"', stat_data[stat_data.find('\"team\"'):stat_data.find('\"team\"')+60] if '\"team\"' in stat_data else '')
        
        bb = bb_match.group(1) if bb_match else 'N/A'
        ab = ab_match.group(1) if ab_match else 'N/A'
        
        # Check if it's Yankees
        if 'Yankees' in stat_data:
            print(f'{name}: BB={bb}, AB={ab} (Yankees)')
        else:
            print(f'{name}: BB={bb}, AB={ab} (not Yankees or no data)')
    else:
        print(f'{name}: not found')
"

import urllib.request, re

print('Yankees' if 'Yankees' in data else 'Not Yankees')

python3 -c "
import urllib.request, re

players_search = [
    ('Lou Piniella', 'Lou%20Piniella'),
    ('Cliff Johnson', 'Cliff%20Johnson'),
    ('Paul Blair', 'Paul%20Blair'),
    ('Bucky Dent', 'Bucky%20Dent'),
    ('Fred Stanley', 'Fred%20Stanley'),
    ('George Zeber', 'George%20Zeber'),
]

for name, search in players_search:
    try:
        url = f'https://statsapi.mlb.com/api/v1/people/search?names={search}'
        req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0', 'Accept': 'application/json'})
        resp = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10)
        data = resp.read().decode('utf-8')
        id_match = re.search(r'\"id\":(\d+)', data)
        if id_match:
            pid = id_match.group(1)
            stat_url = f'https://statsapi.mlb.com/api/v1/people/{pid}/stats?stats=season&season=1977&group=hitting'
            stat_req = urllib.request.Request(stat_url, headers={'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0', 'Accept': 'application/json'})
            stat_resp = urllib.request.urlopen(stat_req, timeout=10)
            stat_data = stat_resp.read().decode('utf-8')
            bb_match = re.search(r'\"baseOnBalls\":(\d+)', stat_data)
            ab_match = re.search(r'\"atBats\":(\d+)', stat_data)
            bb = bb_match.group(1) if bb_match else 'N/A'
            ab = ab_match.group(1) if ab_match else 'N/A'
            is_yankees = 'Yankees' in stat_data
            print(f'{name}: BB={bb}, AB={ab} {\"(Yankees)\" if is_yankees else \"(not Yankees)\"}')
        else:
            print(f'{name}: ID not found')
    except Exception as e:
        print(f'{name}: Error - {e}')
"

input.summary:
The Yankee with the most walks in the 1977 regular season was **Roy White**, who had 75 walks. That same season, Roy White had **519 at bats**.

This was determined by checking all key Yankees players via the MLB Stats API. The top walks leaders on the 1977 Yankees were:
1. Roy White: 75 BB, 519 AB
2. Reggie Jackson: 74 BB, 525 AB  
3. Graig Nettles: 68 BB, 589 AB
4. Willie Randolph: 64 BB, 551 AB

[Source: MLB Stats API (statsapi.mlb.com), 1977 season hitting stats for New York Yankees players]
